title: Maid exits with `0` with invalid switch
author: coaxial
body:
**👋 Before reporting a bug, please make sure you're running the latest available version of maid (`gem install maid`).**

**👋 If you're using Ruby 2.7+, please make sure you're running maid v0.10.0+ (`gem install maid --pre`)**

**Describe the bug**
Maid exits with return code `0` when using an invalid switch.

**To Reproduce**
`maid -version` (note only one `-`)

**Expected behavior**
Output help and exit `1`

**Logs**
If applicable, add logs to help explain your problem. You can find the logs at `~/.maid/maid.log`.

**Environment (please complete the information):**
- Maid version (`maid --version`): `0.11.1`
- OS, distribution (Linux), and version: Mint 22.1
- Ruby version (`ruby --version`): `3.3.7`

**Minimal `rules.rb` to reproduce the issue:**
```ruby
# Rule(s)
```

**Additional context**
```
$ maid -version                                                                                                                               
Unknown switches "-version"                                                                                                                                               
Deprecation warning: Thor exit with status 0 on errors. To keep this behavior, you must define `exit_on_failure?` in `Maid::App`                                          
You can silence deprecations warning by setting the environment variable THOR_SILENCE_DEPRECATION. 
```

- Ruby version (`ruby --version`): `ruby 3.3.7 (2025-01-15 revision be31f993d7) [x86_64-linux]`

**Minimal `rules.rb` to reproduce the issue:**
```ruby
# Rule(s)
```

**Additional context**
Related to #337

title: `maid clean -n` outputs `undefined method 'new' for false` for `::Maid::Logger`
author: christymcgrory
body:
**👋 Before reporting a bug, please make sure you're running the latest available version of maid (`gem install maid`).**
✅

**👋 If you're using Ruby 2.7+, please make sure you're running maid v0.10.0+ (`gem install maid --pre`)**
✅

**Describe the bug**
When trying to launch maid for the first time, I get an error. ~Running with sudo seems to fix the issue~, but I couldn't find anything about it in the docs. I assume it's something to do with the fact that rbenv shims maid to `/Users/dev/.rbenv/shims/maid`. Both the `maid` bin and `~/.maid/maid.log` are owned by me .

**To Reproduce**
`rbenv global 3.4.2`
`gem install maid --pre`
`maid sample`
`mv ~/.maid/rules.sample.rb ~/.maid/rules.rb`
`maid clean -n`

**Expected behavior**
The app runs 

**Logs**
```sh
/Users/dev/.rbenv/versions/3.4.0/lib/ruby/gems/3.4.0+1/gems/maid-0.11.0/lib/maid/maid.rb:31:in 'Maid::Maid#initialize': undefined method 'new' for false (NoMethodError)

    @logger = options[:logger].new(device: options[:log_device])
                              ^^^^
        from /Users/dev/.rbenv/versions/3.4.0/lib/ruby/gems/3.4.0+1/gems/maid-0.11.0/lib/maid/app.rb:35:in 'Class#new'
        from /Users/dev/.rbenv/versions/3.4.0/lib/ruby/gems/3.4.0+1/gems/maid-0.11.0/lib/maid/app.rb:35:in 'Maid::App#clean'
        from /Users/dev/.rbenv/versions/3.4.0/lib/ruby/gems/3.4.0+1/gems/thor-1.2.2/lib/thor/command.rb:27:in 'Thor::Command#run'
        from /Users/dev/.rbenv/versions/3.4.0/lib/ruby/gems/3.4.0+1/gems/thor-1.2.2/lib/thor/invocation.rb:127:in 'Thor::Invocation#invoke_command'
        from /Users/dev/.rbenv/versions/3.4.0/lib/ruby/gems/3.4.0+1/gems/thor-1.2.2/lib/thor.rb:392:in 'Thor.dispatch'
        from /Users/dev/.rbenv/versions/3.4.0/lib/ruby/gems/3.4.0+1/gems/thor-1.2.2/lib/thor/base.rb:485:in 'Thor::Base::ClassMethods#start'
        from /Users/dev/.rbenv/versions/3.4.0/lib/ruby/gems/3.4.0+1/gems/maid-0.11.0/bin/maid:5:in '<top (required)>'
        from /Users/dev/.rbenv/versions/3.4.0/bin/maid:25:in 'Kernel#load'
        from /Users/dev/.rbenv/versions/3.4.0/bin/maid:25:in '<main>'
```

**Environment (please complete the information):**
- Maid version (`maid --version`): `0.11.0`
- OS, distribution (Linux), and version: MacOS Sequoia 15.4 (24E248)
- Ruby version (`ruby --version`): `ruby 3.4.2 (2025-02-15 revision d2930f8e7a) +PRISM [arm64-darwin24]`

**Minimal `rules.rb` to reproduce the issue:**
```ruby
rule 'Tag old files in Downloads with red tag' do
  # Find files in Downloads older than a month
  dir('~/Downloads/*').select do |path|
    File.file?(path) && File.mtime(path) < 1.month.ago
  end.each do |file|
    # Note: This requires the "tag" command to be installed
    # Install it with: brew install tag
    system("tag -a Red \"#{file}\"")
    # log "Tagged as Red: #{file}"
  end
end
```

title: `code-coverage` action doesn't work on forks
author: coaxial
body:
**Describe the bug**
The `code-coverage` action fails to run on PRs from users who aren't collaborators of the project.

**To Reproduce**
1. Fork project
2. Make a PR
3. See `code-coverage` fail because `CC_TEST_REPORTER_ID` env var is missing

**Expected behavior**
It should run without errors.

**Logs**
https://github.com/maid/maid/actions/runs/5775015686/job/15704394069?pr=323/

**Environment (please complete the information):**
- Maid version (`maid --version`): `v0.10.0`
- OS, distribution (Linux), and version: GH Actions
- Ruby version (`ruby --version`): `3.2`

**Minimal `rules.rb` to reproduce the issue:**
N/A

**Additional context**
Discussion on workarounds: https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/63116

title: Tests routinely write to `~` and don't clean up after themselves
author: coaxial
body:
This was not a problem while using FakeFS. But FakeFS is being phased out (see #315).

Many test write to `~` which clobbers the developer's home directory if running `rspec` directly.

As a work-around, the test suite will refuse to run outside of Docker or Vagrant (see https://github.com/maid/maid/wiki/Contributing#use-docker-or-vagrant)

Writing files to `~` for testing purposes should be replaced with a safer alternative: `/tmp/maid-specs`.

This is to track the gradual replacement of every write to `~` instead of `/tmp/maid-specs` and the systematic cleanup of any written files during the test using an `after` hook.

title: FakeFS causes flaky tests
author: coaxial
body:
FakeFS is convenient because it gives a clean, in-memory filesystem for every test without the need to clean up afterwards.

However, it doesn't implement file locking (`File#flock`) for instance which causes issues with the standard library, and doesn't fully behave like an actual filesystem.

This is to track the removal of FakeFS from all tests.

title: Ruby compiled from source in Vagrant box
author: coaxial
body: It is unfortunate that Ruby gets compiled from source for every `vagrant up` rather than using pre-compiled binaries... It takes almost 10 mins to bring the box up on my machine.

title: Geocoder tests fail on GH Actions
author: coaxial
body:
**👋 Before reporting a bug, please make sure you're running the latest available version of maid (`gem install maid`).**

**👋 If you're using Ruby 2.7+, please make sure you're running maid v0.10.0+ (`gem install maid --pre`)**

**Describe the bug**
Geocoder makes live HTTP requests, which have started to fail on CI on ubuntu-2004 for some reason.

**To Reproduce**
Run the CI pipline on `ubuntu-2004` for Ruby versions 2.7 to 3.2. Note that it works if run locally, only fails on the GH worker.

**Expected behavior**
All tests should pass.

**Logs**
From https://github.com/maid/maid/actions/runs/4764486552/jobs/8469129838

```
Failures:

  1) Dependency expectations Geocoder translates latitude and longitude into street addresses
     Failure/Error: expect(city).to eq(['Sydney'])

       expected: ["Sydney"]
            got: []

       (compared using ==)
     # ./spec/dependency_spec.rb:101:in `block (3 levels) in <top (required)>'

Finished in 7.46 seconds (files took 0.82527 seconds to load)
186 examples, 1 failure

Failed examples:

rspec ./spec/dependency_spec.rb:99 # Dependency expectations Geocoder translates latitude and longitude into street addresses

/opt/hostedtoolcache/Ruby/3.2.2/x64/bin/ruby -I/home/runner/work/maid/maid/vendor/bundle/ruby/3.2.0/gems/rspec-core-3.12.2/lib:/home/runner/work/maid/maid/vendor/bundle/ruby/3.2.0/gems/rspec-support-3.12.0/lib /home/runner/work/maid/maid/vendor/bundle/ruby/3.2.0/gems/rspec-core-3.12.2/exe/rspec --pattern spec/\*\*\{,/\*/\*\*\}/\*_spec.rb failed
```

**Environment (please complete the information):**
- Maid version (`maid --version`): `0.10.0-alpha.3`
- OS, distribution (Linux), and version: `ubuntu-2004` on GH Actions
- Ruby version (`ruby --version`): 2.7 to 3.2

**Minimal `rules.rb` to reproduce the issue:**
N/A

**Additional context**
This doesn't seem to be tied to a particular Ruby version (or a particular Ubuntu version, maybe?) -- It could be a rate limit on API requests coming from the GH workers because the tests sometimes pass and sometimes not (in the test matrix).

title: `Maid::Tools` is very large
author: coaxial
body:
Splitting it in smaller modules would make it easier to parse it.

Maybe something like `Tools::Operations` for moving/renaming/deleting/copying,
`Tools::FileMetadata` for created_at, size_of, etc, `Tools::OSX` for
OSX-only methods...

title: README might benefit from getting refreshed
author: coaxial
body: The README file could benefit from more up-to-date information and roadmap so new users don't think this is an abandoned project.

title: Error message for `watch` can be confusing
author: coaxial
body: `watch` could show a friendlier error message instead of blowing up if the path is invalid. See #159 for context.

title: Methods aren't all YARD documented
author: coaxial
body:
Methods should have a YARD-style documentation comment before them to
make it easier to grok the code and use automated tools to refactor/get
hints.

title: Gem `dimensions` is seemingly abandoned
author: coaxial
body:
It could maybe be replaced with
[fastimage](https://github.com/sdsykes/fastimage) which also allows for
remote images.

title: OSX specific commands aren't easily spotable
author: coaxial
body:
There are a few Maid rules methods that will only work on OSX and not
Linux.

Since the gem supports both platforms, the OSX-exclusive commands should
be renamed to make it obvious they're OSX only.

title: `deprecated` gem is abandoned
author: coaxial
body:
The repo for it is no longer available, neither is the documentation.

`bundle open deprecated` shows that the code isn't all that complicated
and could probably be rolled into Maid itself to avoid depending on this
gem.

title: Dead code isn't tracked
author: coaxial
body: This could be useful: https://github.com/danmayer/coverband
